note: Formal round upright minuscule, which recalls the “Perlschrift”
Small and elegant, upright early minuscule. Simple initials introduce each troparion and are located outside the text block. Titles are written in Alexandrian majuscule

One bifolio of the present manuscript is constituted of one original folio, measured approximately 200x150 mm. The Schriftspiegel is approximately 155x90 mm, with large margins: the upper measures 35 mm (f. 185v), the outer 55 mm (f. 185v), and the lower 40 mm (f. 184r)
Prickings are still visible in the outer margin. They are elongated rather than round, probably made with a penknife. The ruled lines correspond to Sautel-Leroy type 00A1 (Rossetto)

text_unit.label: Troparia for Saint Macrina the Younger

work_wit.alt_title: Troparia for Saint Macrina the Younger

note.value: The folios contain troparia (one kathisma and four stichera) to be performed on the feast of Saint Macrina the Younger, sister of Saint Basil the Great and Gregory of Nyssa, on 19th July (see MR VI 159–168) (Rossetto)
Description by Nigel Wilson, Giulia Rossetto

Bibliography: Abbreviation for the edition, MR VI = Μηναῖα τοῦ ὅλου ἐνιαουτοῦ, VI, Ἐν Ῥώμῃ 1901 ; G. Rossetto, Greek Palimpsests at Saint Catherine's Monastery (Sinai): Three Euchologia as Case Studies, Vienna 2022

note: Late 9th – early 10th c.  CE (Rossetto) / Late 10th-11th c. (Wilson) CE

note.value: Reconstructed folio order, ff. 185v+184r, 185r+184v (Rossetto)
Description of undertext by Nigel Wilson, Giulia Rossetto

Bibliography: Abbreviation for the edition, MR VI = Μηναῖα τοῦ ὅλου ἐνιαουτοῦ, VI, Ἐν Ῥώμῃ 1901 ; G. Rossetto, Greek Palimpsests at Saint Catherine's Monastery (Sinai): Three Euchologia as Case Studies, Vienna 2022

note: Upright, round, calligraphic minuscule with thick, pronounced strokes. The writing is pendent from the ruled lines. Each ode begins with an initial in epigraphischer Auszeichnungsmajuskel
The writing runs parallel to the overtext, rotated through 180°
Each side of the bifolio preserves 18 to 20 lines of text, 6 mm apart and arranged in one column

A single folio of Sin. gr. 962 corresponds to a single folio of the original manuscript, and hence the original manuscript was probably of similar dimensions: 150x100 mm. The Schriftspiegel is approximately 110x70 mm, with an outer margin of 25 mm and a lower of 20 mm

note.value: The folios contain an unknown kanon on Saint Nicholas. It is not clear whether the folios belonged to a Menaion, an Oktoechos, or a manuscript intended for private devotion (Rossetto)
Description by Nigel Wilson, Giulia Rossetto
Bibliography: Rossetto 2023a
Reconstructed folio order, ff. 186v, 186r, 183v, 183r (Rossetto)
The two folios do not contain a consecutive text. At least one bifolio is missing in-between.
Most recent layer of double palimpsest
Description of undertext by Nigel Wilson, Giulia Rossetto
Bibliography: Rossetto 2023a

note: One bifolio of the present manuscript is made of one original folio, which measured approximately 200x150 mm. The Schriftspiegel is approximately 170x120 mm, with an upper margin of 20 mm (f. 186r), an outer of 30 mm (f. 183v), and a lower of 45 mm (f. 183v)

note: Elegant, very small upright minuscule, resembling the earliest examples of “Perlschrift”
One folio of Sin. gr. 962 corresponds to one quarter of the original folio with the Chrysostom text. The original manuscript was of large size, measuring approximately 300x200 mm

location.repository: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai

note.value: Leather on wood, back and front cover consist of two pieces of leather; visible fabric layer, no pastedowns. Binding partially missing, spine exposed
Codex with damaged spine
Quire signatures preserved at the beginning of each quire, except the first and the seventh | Presence of reinforcement strips at the beginning, end, and middle of most quires
Palimpsest is only in Quire 23 (ff. 182-187), which is more recent than the rest of the manuscript (13th c.), although the overtext is continuous
